text: The rain in Neo-Tokyo tasted of static and regret. It hammered against the chrome and plasteel of the towering advertisements – colossal koi fish leaping through digital waterfalls, hawking memory implants and synth-sushi. Below, the alleyways pulsed with a sickly green light, choked with the detritus of a thousand broken dreams. Citizens moved like ghosts, their bodies augmented, their faces obscured by data-overlays. A young man, Kai, slipped through the shadows, a dark leather jacket clinging to his wiry frame.

His movements were precise, economical, honed by years spent navigating the city’s undercurrents. Security drones, sleek and predatory, patrolled the perimeter, their optical sensors scanning for anomalies. He ignored them, a ripple of code blurring his presence. Kai wasn't interested in detection; he was hunting a ghost – a fragmented trace of data belonging to the vanished corporate magnate, Silas Vance. The shard pulsed with a malevolent blue light, a captured echo of Vance’s illicit dealings.
